requires: configured SQLite database is canonical Case and Turn state
canonical writes use explicit-approval transactional commands with read-back

entries.reason: Replace mutable Facebook-root and date-scoped identity with stable Case and Turn keys.
entries.preserved: SQLite schema version 1, case-local Turn IDs and parent graph, explicit write approval, transactional rollback, committed read-back, and Facebook-access restrictions.
entries.introduced: Globally sequential Case IDs, multiple Cases per Facebook root, root candidate lookup, and deterministic Turn duplicate identities.
entries.validation: The candidate permits separate same-root reply branches, keeps explicit Case-ID selection definitive, covers null-parent root Turns, and excludes latest-reply identity.

entries.reason: Give the mandatory readiness command a plain operational name and keep its canonical Markdown scope explicit.
entries.preserved: The pre-case readiness gate, SQLite schema and integrity checks, canonical Case and Turn state, and external-write restrictions.
entries.introduced: The check command validates canonical repository Markdown and configured SQLite before case work.
entries.validation: The old command name has no current reference or alias, while the readiness gate continues to hash configured repository Markdown before checking SQLite.

entries.reason: Avoid redundant readiness work on ordinary reads and prevent root permalinks from being presented as latest-comment links.
entries.preserved: Readiness validation before canonical writes and after failures, exact supplied URL preservation, and the rule that mutable latest-Turn state is never identity.
entries.introduced: Read-only queries may reuse fresh evidence, and open-Case presentation reports the latest public Turn permalink or an explicit missing-link state without root fallback.
entries.validation: The open-Case producer selects public Turns only, exposes the selected Turn ID, reports missing URLs explicitly, and skills place readiness checks immediately before approved writes rather than routine reads.

entries.reason: Prevent redundant readiness checks during ordinary read-only queries when fresh sufficient evidence already exists.
entries.preserved: Mandatory readiness checks before canonical writes, after failures, and whenever database state is uncertain.
entries.introduced: Ordinary read-only queries must reuse fresh sufficient readiness evidence rather than optionally rerunning the check.
entries.validation: The rule retains every write and uncertainty gate while making the previously optional read-only reuse behavior mandatory.

entries.reason: A recommended public reply was delivered as staged fragments and was later expanded using private planning instead of as one complete response to the supplied public exchange.
entries.preserved: Reply brevity, one-pivotal-point focus, verified external fact use, and the follow-up skill's public-context boundary remain unchanged.
entries.introduced: DL-REPLY-01 requires complete ready-to-post text for the supplied post and target comment chain unless the user requests partial or staged content.
entries.validation: Replayed the fragmentary-reply and private-context failures; the rule requires a complete public-exchange reply while retaining user-requested fragments and verified external facts.
entries.source_history: Targeted Git history was used because no prior local AGENTS.history.json existed.

entries.reason: The user required every proposed public Dialogue Lab reply to be complete and ready to post, without a partial-or-staged exception.
entries.preserved: Self-contained ready-to-post output remains required; fact verification and public-context isolation remain separate in the follow-up skill.
entries.introduced: DL-REPLY-01 now unconditionally requires one complete, self-contained, ready-to-post response.
entries.validation: Replayed full and partial reply outputs; a complete ready-to-post response passes, while partial or staged output fails regardless of request wording.
entries.source_history: Prior structured local history and the related global OUT-03 history were consulted.

entries.reason: Apply the approved complete-public-reply rule and public-context boundary to release/local rebuilt from current main.
entries.preserved: All shipped Case and Turn identity rules, earlier history records, current global output brevity, fact verification, and unrelated Dialogue Lab behavior remain unchanged.
entries.introduced: The final DL-REPLY-01 wording and matching follow-up skill boundary coexist with the shipped identity model on release/local.
entries.validation: Current global OUT-03 and every existing local rule were re-read; concise output remains compatible with one complete ready-to-post public reply, and no prior history relation was rewritten.
entries.source_history: The uncommitted full-reply-rule history, shipped local history, and current global OUT-03 history were consulted.
